title: Wheel of Fortune Tarot Card Meaning & Best Crystals
name: Wheel of Fortune
theme: Cycles, change, the turning of fortune, momentum
upright_meaning: A great wheel turns, surrounded by winged figures — a sphinx above, a snake descending, a jackal-headed figure rising — with the four fixed signs of the zodiac in the corners reading books. The Wheel of Fortune is the archetype of cycles — the recognition that nothing stays the same, that what is up will turn and what is down will rise. Upright, it speaks to momentum, to a turning point, and to the wisdom of riding change rather than resisting it.
reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Wheel often points to resistance to change — holding on to a phase that has ended, or a streak where momentum feels against you. It is not a verdict of bad luck; it is an invitation to ask what you are refusing to release, and what is actually within your influence versus what is not.
psychological_lens: locus of control (the distinction between what you can shape and what you must accept as outside your control)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Wheel resonates with the Eastern recognition of impermanence and cyclic existence — not as fatalism, but as the freedom that comes from no longer clinging to a single fixed outcome.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Turquoise, carried for protection across the Silk Road's turning fortunes, embodies the traveler's steady heart through change — fortune's companion, not its victim.

recommended_practice: sort one current situation into 'within my influence' and 'outside my control,' and act only on the first column

best_overall.name: Green Aventurine
best_overall.reason: Green Aventurine is the stone of opportunity and the willingness to move with change. The Wheel turns whether you resist or not — hold Aventurine when you need to meet a turning point with openness rather than grip, trusting your own response within the cycle.

best_upright.name: Citrine
best_upright.reason: Citrine carries the abundant, Jupiter-ruled energy of fortune's upturn. For an upright Wheel draw, it supports optimism and the readiness to act when momentum is with you.

best_reversed.name: Obsidian
best_reversed.reason: When The Wheel reverses into resistance or stuckness, Obsidian helps surface the hidden pattern you are clinging to. It is the stone for honest shadow-work around what you refuse to release.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz steadies The Wheel's love readings, where the phase of a relationship may be turning. It supports meeting change in partnership with heart, not panic.

best_daily_wear.name: Turquoise
best_daily_wear.reason: Turquoise has been carried for protection and good fortune across cultures for millennia. Worn daily, it is a steady companion through fortune's ups and downs — a reminder that you are the traveler, not the wheel.

source_notes: 四源共识=Aventurine(opportunity 最贴 Wheel 的'cycles/turning'archetype)；best_reversed=Obsidian(逆位 resistance 时 shadow work)

content:
When the Wheel of Fortune appears in a reading, it often marks a turning point — a cycle completing, a change arriving, the recognition that nothing stays the same. Card 10 of the Major Arcana, it is the archetype of cycles. Paired with the right crystals, this card becomes an invitation to meet change with openness rather than grip, and to sort honestly what is within your influence from what is simply turning.

Wheel of Fortune at a Glance

The Wheel of Fortune is the card of cycles, change, and turning points. Upright, it points to momentum and a shift in fortune; reversed, it often asks where you are resisting a necessary change. The crystal most often paired with the Wheel is Green Aventurine, the stone of opportunity and moving with change.

  • Archetype: The Turning
  • Core theme: Cycles, change, the turning of fortune, momentum
  • Recommended crystal: Green Aventurine
  • Upright: cycles, change, destiny
  • Reversed: resistance to change, bad luck streak, stagnation

Wheel of Fortune Upright: Meaning & Message

In the Rider-Waite image, a great wheel turns in the sky, surrounded by winged figures — a sphinx seated above it, a snake descending on the left, a jackal-headed figure rising on the right. Written around the wheel's rim are the letters TARO interspersed with Hebrew letters spelling a divine name. In the four corners sit the fixed signs of the zodiac — the angel (Aquarius), the eagle (Scorpio), the lion (Leo), the bull (Taurus) — each reading from a book. The wheel turns whether anyone wills it or not. Upright, the Wheel of Fortune is the archetype of cycles. It is the recognition, found across every spiritual and philosophical tradition, that nothing stays fixed — what is up will turn, what is down will rise, and the only constant is change itself. In everyday terms, the Wheel often appears at a genuine turning point: a phase completing, an unexpected opening, a shift in circumstances that you did not orchestrate and cannot fully control. This card is neither purely lucky nor purely ominous. It simply says: the wheel is turning. The work is to meet the turn well — to act on what is within your influence and to release your grip on what is not.

Wheel of Fortune Reversed: The Shadow Aspect

When the Wheel appears reversed, the relationship to change has gone somewhere difficult. The familiar form is resistance — clinging to a phase that has already ended, holding a position the wheel has moved past, refusing to let the turn happen. A second form is a streak where momentum feels against you — not a curse, but the natural downward arc of a cycle, asking patience rather than panic. This is not a forecast of bad luck. A reversed card is information for reflection. The Wheel reversed often asks one honest question: what are you refusing to release — and what would change if you sorted the situation into what you can actually shape and what you must simply let turn?

A reflection prompt: Sort one current situation into two columns: what is genuinely within your influence, and what is outside your control. Commit to acting only on the first column this week. The Wheel asks you to ride the turn, not to fight it.

Best Crystals for Wheel of Fortune

Crystals paired with the Wheel of Fortune tend to share qualities of opportunity, flow, or protection through change, matching the card's theme of cycles and turning points. The five below are drawn from the major crystal-and-tarot references and mineral tradition, chosen for distinct aspects of this card — from the opportunity stone itself, to a stone of abundant optimism, to a stone that surfaces what you are resisting.

Best Overall Crystal for Wheel of Fortune

Green Aventurine is the stone of opportunity and the willingness to move with change. The Wheel turns whether you resist or not — hold Aventurine when you need to meet a turning point with openness rather than grip, trusting your own response within the cycle even when you cannot control the cycle itself.

The Wheel turns whether you resist or not — hold Aventurine when you need to meet a turning point with openness rather than grip, trusting your own response within the cycle. Best Crystal for Upright Wheel of Fortune Energy

Citrine carries the abundant, Jupiter-ruled energy of fortune's upturn. For an upright Wheel draw, when momentum is genuinely shifting in your favor, Citrine supports optimism and the readiness to act when the window opens — the willingness to step into the turn rather than hesitate past it.

Citrine — Citrine carries the abundant, Jupiter-ruled energy of fortune's upturn. For an upright Wheel draw, it supports optimism and the readiness to act when momentum is with you. Explore Citrine in depth → · Browse Citrine jewelry →

Best Crystal for Reversed Wheel of Fortune (Shadow Work)

When the Wheel reverses into resistance or stuckness, Obsidian helps surface the hidden pattern you are clinging to. It is the stone for honest shadow-work around what you refuse to release — the belief, the role, the identity whose time has turned and that you are still holding in place by force.

Obsidian — When The Wheel reverses into resistance or stuckness, Obsidian helps surface the hidden pattern you are clinging to. It is the stone for honest shadow-work around what you refuse to release. Obsidian properties & symbolism → · Find your Obsidian piece →

Best Crystal for Love & Relationship Readings

Rose Quartz steadies the Wheel's love readings, where the phase of a relationship may be turning — deepening, shifting, or completing. It supports meeting the change in partnership with heart rather than panic, so the connection rides the turn consciously rather than being broken by it.

Rose Quartz — Rose Quartz steadies The Wheel's love readings, where the phase of a relationship may be turning. It supports meeting change in partnership with heart, not panic. Read Rose Quartz Meaning → · Shop Rose Quartz →

Best Crystal to Wear Daily

Turquoise has been carried for protection and good fortune across cultures for millennia — across the Silk Road, in Native American traditions, in Tibetan and Persian jewelry. Worn daily, it is a steady companion through fortune's ups and downs, a reminder that you are the traveler, not the wheel.

Turquoise — Turquoise has been carried for protection and good fortune across cultures for millennia. Worn daily, it is a steady companion through fortune's ups and downs — a reminder that you are the traveler, not the wheel. Browse Turquoise jewelry →

A Crystal Practice for Wheel of Fortune

A short practice to bring Wheel of Fortune's energy into your reading or your day. Keep it practical — crystals are a focus for intention, not magic.

You'll need: Green Aventurine or Citrine.

Step 1 — Set the focus (1 min): Take one current situation that feels like it is turning, and sort it into two columns: what is genuinely within your influence, and what is outside your control. Be honest — most of us exaggerate the first column and resent the second.

Step 2 — Hold the stone + draw a card (2 min): Hold your Green Aventurine over your heart and draw a card with the question "how can I move with this change rather than against it?" Let the stone anchor you in openness as you sit with whatever the card surfaces about your own response to the turn.

Step 3 — Close with one commitment: Close by committing to one concrete action — only on the "within my influence" column — that you will take this week to meet the turn well. Place the stone somewhere you will see it until the action is done. The Wheel does not reward fighting it; it rewards riding it.

Prefer something quicker? Touch your Turquoise jewelry each morning and ask: "What is turning today that I can meet with openness instead of resistance?" The card's gift is the wisdom to ride change; this small ritual trains the habit of responding rather than gripping.

Crystals to Support Wheel of Fortune Reversed

When Wheel of Fortune appears reversed, it often points to resistance to change, bad luck streak, stagnation — the card's energy in shadow rather than a bad omen. It's an invitation to notice where cycles and change has gone into overdrive or gone quiet. The crystal below is traditionally used to support this specific reflection.

Supporting stone — Obsidian: Obsidian is the steady stone for the shadow work this reversal invites. When you notice yourself clinging to a phase that has turned, hold a piece and let its unflinching mirror surface the pattern beneath the resistance — often a fear of what the next phase will ask of you. Pair it with the Turquoise above; together they support releasing the grip while staying steady through the turn.

Wheel of Fortune Through Three Lenses

1. Tarot tradition: In the Rider-Waite tradition, the Wheel of Fortune is numbered 10 and corresponds to Jupiter. It is read as the archetype of cycles, fate, and turning points — the recognition that fortune rises and falls beyond any individual's control. The four fixed signs of the zodiac in the corners, each reading a book, mark the stability that exists behind the turning: the patterns that endure even as circumstances change.

2. Psychological archetype: In psychological terms, the Wheel maps onto the well-studied concept of locus of control — the distinction between what we believe we can shape (internal locus) and what we attribute to outside forces (external locus). Research consistently finds that well-being depends less on what actually happens than on the accuracy of this sorting: acting on what you can influence, and accepting what you cannot. Read through this lens, the Wheel is not fatalistic. It is asking you to sort honestly — to put your energy where it can actually move something, and to release the rest.

This lens treats Wheel of Fortune as a mirror for self-inquiry — the psychological dimension here is locus of control — rather than a prediction.

3. Crystal companion: As a crystal companion, Green Aventurine becomes the tangible reminder that you can meet change with openness even when you cannot control it. The Wheel reminds you that the turn is coming whether you resist or not; the stone gives you a focus for the open-handed response that riding a turn actually requires. Worn as jewelry, it is a small daily cue to stay receptive — to look for the opportunity inside the change rather than only the loss.

Wheel of Fortune in the Eastern Tradition

The Wheel resonates directly with the Eastern recognition of impermanence and cyclic existence — a central teaching across both Buddhist and Hindu traditions. Importantly, this is not fatalism: the Eastern understanding of cyclical existence is not "nothing matters, so give up," but rather "nothing is fixed, so there is freedom in how you meet what turns." The very word for the cycle of existence, samsara, literally means "wandering" or "going around" — the wheel. For the Wheel of Fortune, this echo reframes change not as threat but as the condition that makes any genuine freedom possible.

Turquoise, carried for protection and good fortune across the turning fortunes of the Silk Road for thousands of years, embodies the traveler's steady heart through change. The stone has been prized in Tibetan, Persian, Navajo, and many other traditions precisely for the quality the Wheel asks: the steady companionship that lets you ride the turns of fortune without being defined by them. For this card, the cross-cultural reverence for turquoise as the traveler's stone pairs naturally with the Western crystal-and-tarot reading of it as a stone of steady fortune.

A Mini Crystal Tarot Spread for Wheel of Fortune

A three-card spread to explore Wheel of Fortune's message in your life, with a crystal paired to each position.

PositionQuestionCompanion Crystal
1. Where Wheel of Fortune is showing upWhere is The Turning energy active in my life right now?Green Aventurine
2. What it invitesWhat is this card asking me to notice or do?Citrine
3. The next stepWhat small action aligns me with this card's healthy expression?Turquoise

Shuffle your deck, place a stone on each position as you draw, and sit with the three cards together for a few minutes before journaling.

Tarot and crystal meanings are based on spiritual traditions, symbolism, and personal mindfulness practices. They are a tool for self-reflection and contemplation, not a substitute for medical, financial, or professional advice — and not a prediction of fixed outcomes.

Frequently Asked Questions

What does Wheel of Fortune mean in tarot?The Wheel of Fortune (card 10 of the Major Arcana) is the card of cycles, change, and turning points. It corresponds to Jupiter and is traditionally read as the archetype of fortune's turning — the recognition that what rises will fall and what falls will rise. In a reading, it often points to a shift in circumstances, a phase completing, or a change that is arriving whether you will it or not.
What is the upright meaning of Wheel of Fortune?Upright, the Wheel of Fortune points to a turning point and a shift in momentum. It suggests that a cycle is completing or beginning, that change is in motion, and that the work is to meet the turn with openness rather than resistance. It is often read as a sign of movement — sometimes favorable, sometimes simply necessary — and an invitation to act on what is within your influence.
What are the best crystals for Wheel of Fortune?The crystals most often paired with the Wheel of Fortune are Green Aventurine (for opportunity and flow), Citrine (for the abundant optimism of a favorable turn), Obsidian (for surfacing resistance to change), Rose Quartz (to steady love through a turning phase), and Turquoise (the traveler's stone of steady fortune). The principle across references is support for meeting change well.
What does Wheel of Fortune mean in love?In love readings, the Wheel of Fortune often points to a turning phase in a relationship — a deepening, a shift, or sometimes a completion that the cycle is bringing. It can also signal an unexpected meeting that arrives with the turn. The invitation is to meet the change with heart rather than to grip the relationship into a shape the cycle has moved past.
What does Wheel of Fortune reversed mean?Reversed, the Wheel of Fortune often points to resistance to change — clinging to a phase that has ended, or a streak where momentum feels against you. It is not a forecast of bad luck — it is an invitation to ask what you are refusing to release, and to sort honestly what is within your influence from what you must simply let turn.
What chakra is Wheel of Fortune associated with?The crystals paired with the Wheel of Fortune work primarily with the Heart and Solar Plexus chakras — Green Aventurine, Rose Quartz, and Turquoise for the open-hearted flow of the Heart, and Citrine for the confident agency of the Solar Plexus. The card's energy lives where openness and willpower meet.
Is Wheel of Fortune a bad card?In tarot, no card is inherently 'good' or 'bad' — every card, including reversed positions, is read as information for reflection rather than a fixed outcome. For the Wheel of Fortune, the card's "change and cycles" theme can read as bad luck or stagnation when reversed — but the invitation is simply to release resistance to a turn that is already happening, not to treat the reversed card as a forecast of misfortune.
Can tarot predict the future?No — tarot is a symbolic system used for self-reflection and contemplation, not a tool that predicts fixed outcomes. The cards surface patterns and questions; your choices shape what happens next. For the Wheel of Fortune, the card may point to a change that is turning — but how you meet that change, and what you choose to do within it, remains entirely in your hands.

  • Death: The Transformer — Endings, transformation, the clearing that makes space for rebirth
  • Judgment: The Awakened — Reckoning, rebirth, hearing the call to a larger life
  • All Tarot Card Crystals → (Hub)

The Wheel of Fortune is the card of the turn that comes to every life. Pair it with a crystal that supports openness through change, and let it be a reminder that you are the traveler on the wheel, not the wheel itself — and that the freedom you have is not to stop the turning, but to choose how you ride it.
